records.ety: [L. consistorium a place of assembly, the place where the emperor's council met, fr. consistere: cf. F. consistoire, It. consistorio. See Consist.]

defs: Primarily, a place of standing or staying together; hence, any solemn assembly or council.
quotes: To council summons all his mighty peers, Within thick clouds and dark tenfold involved, A gloomy consistory.

defs: The spiritual court of a diocesan bishop held before his chancellor or commissioner in his cathedral church or elsewhere.
An assembly of prelates; a session of the college of cardinals at Rome.
quotes: Pius was then hearing of causes in consistory.

defs: A church tribunal or governing body.
notes: ☞ In some churches, as the Dutch Reformed in America, a consistory is composed of the minister and elders of an individual church, corresponding to a Presbyterian church session, and in others, as the Reformed church in France, it is composed of ministers and elders, corresponding to a presbytery. In some Lutheran countries it is a body of clerical and lay officers appointed by the sovereign to superintend ecclesiastical affairs.

defs: Of the nature of, or pertaining to, a consistory.
